title: Pseudo-D: Informing Multi-View Uncertainty Estimation with Calibrated Neural Training Dynamics
summary: The paper proposes 'Pseudo-D', a method to improve uncertainty estimation in medical imaging by generating calibrated pseudo-labels using Neural Network Training Dynamics (NNTD). NNTD tracks a model's learning trajectory across training epochs to quantify sample difficulty, which is then used to create pseudo-labels. This approach enhances performance on tasks like selective classification (knowing when to abstain) and multi-view fusion (integrating information from multiple imaging views). The method was evaluated on a multi-view ultrasound dataset for aortic stenosis classification, demonstrating superior results and better alignment of model uncertainty with input quality. The technique is model-agnostic and easy to integrate.

methodology.name: Pseudo-D
methodology.core_concept: Improving uncertainty estimation by generating calibrated pseudo-labels using Neural Network Training Dynamics (NNTD).
key_techniques: Neural Network Training Dynamics (NNTD): Tracking model predictions across training epochs to measure sample difficulty.
Pseudo-label Generation: Creating labels that quantify uncertainty based on the NNTD trajectory.
Sub-class Calibration: Calibrating pseudo-labels at the sub-class level to handle varying difficulty within classes.
methodology.problem_addressed: The lack of ground-truth labels that capture uncertainty (aleatoric uncertainty) in medical imaging datasets, which are typically single-label.
methodology.focus_uncertainty_type: Aleatoric Uncertainty (AU), the irreducible uncertainty from ambiguous input data.

evaluation.dataset: A challenging multi-view ultrasound dataset for aortic stenosis (AS) classification.
tasks: Selective Classification: Assessing the model's ability to abstain from predictions on uncertain cases.
Multi-view Fusion: Evaluating the model's ability to integrate information from multiple imaging views while accounting for their uncertainties.

key_findings: Training with Pseudo-D improves uncertainty estimation in standard deep learning classifiers.
Pseudo-D outperforms specialized methods on selective classification and multi-view fusion tasks.
The method better aligns model uncertainty with input-specific factors like image quality and anatomical visibility.
Pseudo-D is agnostic to model architecture and requires minimal changes for integration.
